reviews.review_description: The app is amazing but I can’t use it on Oculus Quest 2

So this app has some potential.  The sounds are on point and there is no obnoxious music playing regardless if i turn it on or off, like those other apps.  The scenery is great choices and i do feel like i can relax with the guides.

The bad.
Needs more submersive environments than 180 degrees.  You turn your head naturally and get thrown out of your vibe because of the blue background.  Next, needs a few more environments.  While the default ones are there, some variety would be good.  If i had to pick though, i would want full environment before more partial ones.

reviews.review_title: So bad devs bought fake 5-star reviews.
reviews.review_description: Calm are currently buying fake reviews to try and cover up what must be the poorest attempt at meditation in VR ever. There are much better alternatives already available for the Oculus platform.

I gave it a shot after reading the bad press they got for fake reviews. Yes it is only 180 degrees but for a meditation app which you use while sitting down, it's enough. 
I liked the video which was playing, seemed high resolution. It's a free app. I liked using it.

This is the first meditation I've done in VR. I don't usually use anything but a timer to mediate. Doing it in VR was very engaging. The "debate" over 180-360 video does not apply when you are closing your eyes to relax. It's really neat to teleport to that environment when you open your eyes after relaxing.
High (enough) quality on the videos.
No control over video sound & it's not 360!
Really like the intuitive UI design and straight forward user flow. Although the weight of the headset feels more obvious after I got relax. Overall, I can totally see the big potential of this app.

I am an avid user of the Clam app and Calm Radio. It looks to me that this is just a foot in the door to the VR community. The imagery looks fantastic but the range of view is way too narrow. But again, I’m sure this is just a taste of what is to come.

I actually get more relaxation from calm nature scenes alone, than from listening to guides, therefore it would be nice to have the ambient sounds also without   music  or narration.
180° would be ok, if it were possible to center the view vertically, like every half-decent video player can do it. And 3D should be standard. Apps like Gala relax 5k can do it better. I had a lot of lag in the start menu.
Only 180 which takes you out of the experience.
But needs to be 360 to fully enjoy the environments and experience. also more scenes should be added.

The app is ok for how long i used it, which wasn’t long. There are only 3 scenes, 2 are video with movement and 1 one a picture with no movement (forest). But the killer here is it is only 180. Oh well, it was a freebie. Maybe charge .99 cents and show the complete 360?
There's no way they can claim to be the number 1 meditation app on oculus.

I really love the scenery here- the 3 locations are beautiful but I HATE that they are not 360 degrees and it really ruins the effect of being in the scene for me. I would HAPPILY pay for a version of this app where there are more scenery choices (PLEASE PLEASE allow me to “sit” on a rock by a babbling creek or stream surrounded by green trees, woods, and moss!😍) and please make the scenery 360 degrees and you will have a real winner!

Three simple locations that are not even full VR. No wonder they had to have a massive amount of fake accounts upvote it overnight, because anyone who's actually used it would never give it any kind of higher rating. Avoid this fraudulent "experience".

I liked the visuals, but wished they extended further in the POV.  I almost wonder what's the point of you close your eyes on meditation.  And the audio cut out is too abrupt, it was jarring.  I thought there was an issue.  Maybe keep some white noise going, or fade gradually.
It’s amazing that one game can make you so relaxed

reviews.review_title: A nice translation of the app
reviews.review_description: I use calm on my phone everyday, so I’m really thrilled that I now have calm on my go. Some suggestions though: 1) I’d like to be able to connect to my account, 2) I’d like some unique meditations here - why should they be telling me to close my eyes when I’m using a vr headset,and 3) I’d like the opportunity to do a less or lightly guided meditation in the environments that they’ve created.
great
